title: Overlying cross bar fastener
abstract: A device, a system and a method for attaching a crossbar (8) to a hollow composite pylon (2) where the crossbar (8) is arranged to carry at least one conductor (12), the hollow composite pylon (2), the crossbar (8) and the conductor (12) forming components of a pylon structure (1) for the transmission or distribution of power, and wherein the device comprises an adapter (4), which, in the position of application, is attached to the crossbar (8), is slideable at least into or over the, in the position of application, upper end portion (6) of the hollow composite pylon (2) and mainly along the longitudinal axis (30) of the hollow composite pylon (2) during part of the installation phase.
claims:
Claims C l a i m s 1. A device for attaching a crossbar (8) to a hollow composite pylon (2) where the crossbar (8) is arranged to carry at least one conductor ( 12), the hollow composite pylon (2), the crossbar (8) and the conductor ( 12) forming components of a pylon structure ( 1) for the transmission or distribution of pow Â¬ er, c h a r a c t e r i z e d i n that the device comprises an adapter (4), which, in the position of application, is attached to the crossbar (8), and is slideable at least into or over the, in the position of application, upper portion (6) of the hollow composite pylon (2) and mainly along the longitudinal axis (30) of the hollow composite pylon (2) during part of the installation phase. 2. The device according to claim 1 , wherein the adapter (4) is provided with a fork mount ( 16) for pivotably attaching the adapter (4) to the crossbar (8) . 3. The device according to at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the adapter (4) is formed such that it is at least partially rotatable around the longitudinal axis (30) of the hollow composite pylon (2), in the position of application . 4. The device according to at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the adapter (4) includes an internal body (20) which fits complementarily into the upper portion (6) of the hollow composite pylon (2). 5. The device according to at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the adapter (4) includes an external body (32) which fits complementarily around the upper portion (6) of the hollow composite pylon (2). 6. The device according to at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the adapter (4) includes a guide (22) which is arranged to guide the adapter (4) into the desired position relative to the hollow composite pylon (2). 7. The device according to at least one of the preceding claims, wherein the adapter (4) includes a divided slot (24) extending around the circumference thereof, which is arranged to receive a bolt (26) extending through the adapter (4) and the hollow composite pylon (2) in a radial direction. 

 A system comprising a crossbar (8) attached to a hollow composite pylon (2) and comprising the device in accordance with any one of the preceding claims. . The system according to claim 8, comprising more than one hollow composite pylon (2a, 2b) to which the crossbar (8) is mounted via respective adapters (4), and further comprising a spreader (34) between the upper portions (6) of the hollow composite pylons (2a, 2b). A method for attaching a crossbar (8) to a hollow composite pylon (2) where the crossbar (8) is arranged to carry at least one conductor (12), the hollow composite pylon (2), the crossbar (8) and the conductor ( 12) forming components of a pylon structure (1 ) for the transmission or distribution of power, c h a r a c t e r i z e d i n that an adapter (4), which, in the position of application, is attached to the crossbar (8), is slid at least into or over the, in the position of application, upper portion (6) of the hollow composite pylon (2), mainly along the longitudinal axis (30) of the hollow composite pylon (2), during part of the installation phase. The method according to claim 9, wherein the crossbar (8) is simultaneously connected to more than one hollow composite pylon (2a, 2b), and comprising, before the step of sliding said adapters (4) into or over the upper portion (6) of the hollow composite pylons (2a, 2b), a step of at least temporarily installing a spreader (34) between the upper portions (6) of the hollow composite pylons (2a, 2b).
